ddls:
CREATE TABLE "forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast entry */,
  "point_forecast" REAL /* The point forecast value */,
  "lo_80" REAL /* Lower bound of the 80% confidence interval */,
  "hi_80" REAL /* Upper bound of the 80% confidence interval */,
  "lo_95" REAL /* Lower bound of the 95% confidence interval */,
  "hi_95" REAL /* Upper bound of the 95% confidence interval */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "forecast_time" TEXT /* Exact time of the forecast */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the weather parameter */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the data source */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the location */,
  "forecast_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the forecast (optional, can be updated later) */,
  "forecast_model" TEXT /* Model used for generating the forecast */,
  "forecast_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments related to the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES weather_parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES forecast_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather parameter */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather parameter (e.g., Temperature, Humidity) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather parameter */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the parameter */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum possible value for the parameter */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum possible value for the parameter */,
  "is_primary" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating if the parameter is a primary parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source (e.g., National Weather Service) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of source (e.g., Government, Private) */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the data source */,
  "source_reliability" INTEGER /* Reliability rating of the data source (1-5) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL for the data source (if available) */,
  "source_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, researcher, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last time the user account was updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location (in meters) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)
